title: Frontend: Update TypeScript types for rich score breakdown
description:
Update frontend/src/types/api.ts to mirror the new backend CategoryBreakdown schema introduced in task-8. This must be done before the ScoreGauge component work in task-11, which depends on these types.

--- CHANGES TO frontend/src/types/api.ts ---

1. Add a new CategoryBreakdown interface BEFORE the ScoreBreakdown interface:

```typescript
export interface CategoryBreakdown {
  reasoning: string;               // HOW the score was calculated (formula + inputs)
  gap_analysis: string;            // What is missing to reach maximum points
  improvements: string[];          // 1-3 specific actionable items
  short_learning_path: string[];   // Quick wins: weeks to 1-2 months
  long_learning_path: string[];    // Strategic improvements: 3-6 months
}
```

2. Update ScoreBreakdown — change the justifications field type:

OLD:
```typescript
  justifications: Record<'experience' | 'skills' | 'education' | 'role_seniority', string>;
```

NEW:
```typescript
  justifications: Record<'experience' | 'skills' | 'education' | 'role_seniority', CategoryBreakdown>;
```

All other fields in ScoreBreakdown (experience, skills, education, role_seniority, total, job_fit_adjusted) remain unchanged.

--- ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA ---
- CategoryBreakdown interface is exported from types/api.ts with all five fields typed correctly.
- ScoreBreakdown.justifications uses Record<..., CategoryBreakdown> not Record<..., string>.
- No other interfaces in api.ts are modified.
- TypeScript compiler (pnpm tsc --noEmit from frontend/) reports no type errors introduced by this change. Any pre-existing errors are out of scope.
